ag . Phase 5 & 6: Arrange and Shape the Energy

Phase 5: Arrange the Song

e Use the blueprint to place each part where it belongs.

e Decide when instruments enter, leave, build, or drop away.

e Make sure every section has a purpose and the chorus feels bigger than the verse.

e Remove clutter and preserve contrast.

| 2) Student Rule: Arrange with the blueprint, not random decisions.

Phase 6: Refine Transitions & Energy

Every major section change needs a setup and an arrival.

SETUP EXAMPLES: ARRIVAL EXAMPLES:

e Risers e Impacts

e Reverse cymbals e Crash cymbals

¢ Drum fills e Kick return

e Snare builds e Bass entry

e Filter sweeps ¢ Hook start

¢ Dropouts e New layer on the downbeat

GS Goal: make the song feel connected, not like pasted loops.

| 2) Student Rule: Every section change should guide the listener.

—— STUDENT WORKBOOK * MODULE1 ———

aA YT ena
